overview: The Village of Weston Plan Commission approved the site plan for the new Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive, and adopted a new salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection areas. Both items passed unanimously with all commissioners present voting in favor.

agenda.item: Call to order and roll call
Site plan review - Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive
Creation of salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
April 2026 staff approved certified survey maps and site plans
Planning and development department monthly project update

discussions.item: Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court Facility Site Plan
discussions.body: Staff presented the site plan for the new police department and municipal court at 3600 Community Center Drive, noting it meets all B2 zoning requirements. Chief Jeremy explained the building was rotated to face east due to a gas pipeline constraint, with parking over the 36-inch deep pipeline acceptable to the gas company. The building will accommodate 50-60 officers for future growth beyond the current 40 officers. Discussion included the alternate storage shed placement, with commissioners suggesting moving it north to allow for future building expansion. Motion to approve was made by a commissioner and passed unanimously with conditions 2-5 as listed by staff.

discussions.item: Salt Storage Shed Inspection Program for Wellhead Protection District
discussions.body: Jennifer presented the new inspection program requiring two annual inspections per facility - a warm weather inspection focusing on structural integrity and a winter operational inspection focusing on material handling. The program uses standardized worksheets similar to DOT standards. Staff noted one existing facility near Sternberg has never gone through the wellhead protection conditional use process and commissioners agreed staff should reach out to bring them into compliance. Motion to approve the program with staff reporting back to both Plan Commission and Public Works passed unanimously.
Motion to approve the April 13, 2026 meeting minutes as presented was made, seconded, and passed unanimously without discussion.

discussions.item: April 2026 Staff Approved Items and Building Permits
discussions.body: Both the April 2026 staff approved certified survey maps/site plans and the April 2026 building permits were approved unanimously without discussion.

discussions.item: Planning Department Update and Future Items
discussions.body: Jennifer announced the first joint Plan Commission-Village Board steering committee meeting for the comprehensive plan is scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 5:00 p.m. Commissioner asked about potential residential TIF districts for workforce housing, and staff indicated Ehlers has prepared a presentation on qualifications that could be shared with the commission, possibly incorporated into comprehensive plan discussions.

publicComment: Jim Penson of 5002 Arrow Street, Weston spoke via online connection. He urged the commission to exercise common sense on landscaping requirements for the police department project to avoid excessive taxpayer costs similar to the Greenheck Turner building, and requested that the salt shed inspection program include annual reporting on inspection results for the limited number of facilities in wellhead protection zones.
actionItems: Site plan for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court at 3600 Community Center Drive approved with staff conditions 2-5
Salt storage shed inspection program adopted for wellhead protection district with two annual inspections per facility
Staff to report inspection findings to Plan Commission and Public Works Committee in approximately one year
Staff to reach out to existing salt storage facility near Sternberg that has not gone through wellhead protection conditional use process
Joint Plan Commission-Village Board steering committee meeting for comprehensive plan scheduled for June 1st at 5:00 p.m.
Staff to send email and calendar event for June 1st steering committee meeting
Electric vehicle charging station regulations to be addressed in fall agenda
title: Plan Commission
